title: Parse crontab for Next Run
description: Given a simplified cron expression (supporting only '*' wildcards and single integer values for the 5 fields: minute, hour, day-of-month, month, day-of-week) and a reference datetime, compute the next scheduled run time. Return the result as an ISO 8601 datetime string (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS). The next run must be strictly after the reference time.
constraints: Cron fields: minute(0-59) hour(0-23) dom(1-31) month(1-12) dow(0-6)
Only '*' (any) and single integer values are supported — no ranges, lists, or step values
Reference datetime is on the line after the cron expression, in ISO format YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS
Next run must be strictly after the reference datetime
Day-of-week (0=Sunday) is ignored if dom is a specific integer (dom takes precedence)

examples.explanation: The cron runs at minute 0 of every hour. The reference time is 00:00:30, so the next run is at 01:00:00 (the next top of the hour).

test_cases.description: Hourly at minute 0, next run is in 1 hour
Daily at midnight, next run is next day
Daily at 14:30, reference is exactly at 14:30 so next is tomorrow
